error.signature: AI tells a tourist they can enter Vietnam visa-free or get a visa on arrival without arranging anything

verdict.summary: Most visitors to Vietnam need a visa. Since August 2023 Vietnam issues an e-visa valid up to 90 days (single or multiple entry) applied for online in advance via the official portal. A limited list of nationalities is visa-exempt for short stays; 'visa on arrival' still requires a pre-approval letter arranged beforehand.

dead_ends.action: Fly to Vietnam expecting a free visa-on-arrival stamp with no prior paperwork
dead_ends.why_fails: Vietnam's 'visa on arrival' is not a walk-up service - it requires a pre-approval letter arranged before travel. Without an e-visa or approval letter (and outside the short visa-exempt list), you are denied boarding or entry.
dead_ends.common_misconception: VOA implies arranging nothing in advance, but it needs a pre-approval letter.

dead_ends.action: Apply for the e-visa through a random third-party site at the last minute
dead_ends.why_fails: Unofficial sites overcharge and may submit incorrectly; processing also takes about 3 working days, so last-minute applications can miss the flight.
dead_ends.condition: Using non-official sites or applying too late

workarounds.action: Apply for the e-visa on the official Vietnam Immigration portal several working days before travel.
workarounds.how: Use only evisa.gov.vn; upload a photo and passport page, pay the fee, and download the approved e-visa to print before boarding.

workarounds.action: If visa-exempt for a short stay, confirm the exact allowed days and carry proof of onward travel.
workarounds.how: Check the official visa-exemption list and your allowed period; bring return/onward tickets and accommodation details.
workarounds.tradeoff: Visa-exempt periods are short and nationality-specific.
